narration: Ever wonder why pollen dances in water? It's not magic—it's atoms!

narration: This jittery movement is called Brownian motion, named after botanist Robert Brown.
visual_description: Context visual: This jittery movement is called Brownian motion, named after botanist Robert Brown.
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: This jittery movement is called Brownian motion, named after botanist Robert Brown.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: This jittery movement is called Brownian motion, named after botanist Robert Brown.

narration: Brown noticed that pollen grains zigzagged due to tiny, invisible water molecules colliding with them.
visual_description: Escalation visual: Brown noticed that pollen grains zigzagged due to tiny, invisible water molecules colliding with them.
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: Brown noticed that pollen grains zigzagged due to tiny, invisible water molecules colliding with them.

narration: This was some of the first evidence for atoms, changing how we understand matter!
